label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8

extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay won solo from breakaway on summit finish, distancing SOLER Marc by 43 seconds, while EVENEPOEL Remco preserved 28-second GC lead burning teammates to match MAS Enric and ROGLIČ Primož.
tactical_patterns: VINE Jay systematically won every intermediate climb before final attack, prioritizing polka dots over stage conservation—reveals ambition hierarchy.
EVENEPOEL Remco burned entire Quick-Step train matching rivals without attacking, defensive domestique usage pattern indicates vulnerability awareness.
PEDERSEN Mads infiltrating breakaway on mountain stage for sprint points shows green jersey can be won tactically, not physically.
SOLER Marc closing to 25 seconds then fading suggests pacing miscalculation; VINE Jay's consistent acceleration superiority visible throughout break.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: Won from break after controlling every climb, demonstrates ability to manage both intermediate objectives and final victory simultaneously.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Required full team support just to match MAS and ROGLIČ without gaining time, defensive posture on climb reveals narrow advantage.
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Bridged to within 25 seconds of VINE on final climb, shows stage-winning form returning after previous victory.
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: positive: Worked hard to join break then finished 47 seconds down, commentators note 'refound mojo' suggests climbing form improving.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Took green jersey by infiltrating mountain breakaway for sprint points, tactical maturity beyond pure sprinter profile.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Finished with EVENEPOEL and ROGLIČ on summit finish despite defending team burning resources, climbing durability exceeds expectations.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Matched EVENEPOEL and MAS without requiring visible team support, efficient climbing but no attacking intent shown.
rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Carlos: positive: Finished in top group with GC favorites on decisive mountain stage, confirms legitimate podium threat status.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: Named among riders finishing in lead GC group on category one summit, young Spanish climber holding position.
rider_signals.TAARAMÄE Rein: positive: 'Picked it back up' on final climb to finish third, veteran showing climbing resilience after earlier difficulties.
rider_signals.REICHENBACH Sébastien: positive: Worked to pace PINOT into break then finished top ten, experienced mountain domestique executing dual role.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: 'Legs had woken up' phrasing suggests earlier climbing weakness, inconsistent form pattern emerging for GC contender.
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: Explicitly 'gone' from breakaway on final climb, poor climbing form indicates not competitive for GC.
rider_signals.ARMIRAIL Bruno: positive: Dropped back from break to escort teammates forward, installed three Groupama-FDJ riders showing tactical sophistication.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: 'Did his turn as dutifully as always' for EVENEPOEL, confirms domestique role rather than own ambitions.
